title: Field Effectiveness Specialist
description:
At Johnson \& Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.


As guided by Our Credo, Johnson \& Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson \& Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

**Job Function:**

Supply Chain Planning**Job Sub Function:**

Inventory Management**Job Category:**


Professional**All Job Posting Locations:**


Pomezia, Roma, Italy**Job Description:**

**About MedTech**


Fueled by innovation at the intersection of biology and technology, we’re developing the next generation of smarter, less invasive, more personalized treatments.


Your unique talents will help patients on their journey to wellness. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/medtech

**We are searching for the best talent for a Field Effectiveness Specialist role to be in Pomezia, Italy.**

**Purpose**


Within the team, the resource will support Cycle Count activities and the management of excess stock and returns in the field, with the aim of optimizing the consignment stock held at customer sites.


As part of the Cycle Count process, the resource will support the planning of inventory counts at customer locations within their assigned area and assist the external company (Inventory Srl) during the physical count. They will carry out on‑site reconciliations and quantify any inventory discrepancies emerging from the count, working closely with the Inventory and Customer Service teams. The resource will then review the reconciliation results with the customer in order to recover orders related to shortages, provided the necessary supporting documentation is available. They will complete the resolution of any remaining discrepancies together with the Business and Finance teams, ensuring approval within the deadlines defined by the applicable procedures. Finally, they will ensure accounting alignment by performing the necessary system transactions.


As part of field optimization activities for equipment and instruments, the resource will support pull‑back actions following the closure of the inventory count, agreeing with customers on any excess items to be returned. They will also support the management of expired products identified during the cycle count, ensuring their return to the central warehouse. Additionally, the resource will assist in the overall management and optimization of products in the field, such as retained loan sets and consignment items.

**This role requires domestic travel of up to 75%.**

**As a Field Effectiveness Specialist, you will:**

* Prepare the documentation and activate the operational procedures required for the physical inventory of the warehouse and Consignment Stock.
* Analyze any discrepancies found and share the related reporting.
* Interface with Business Management, Financial Controllers, and Finance in order to obtain the necessary authorizations for recording inventory variances.
* Provide the business with specific reports related to inventory stock levels.
* Use the SMART App to manage counting activities and inventory movements.
* Support the Inventory team with Audit activities aimed at verifying inventory operations.
* Guide the business on opportunities for redeployment of company assets using corporate analytical tools.
* Monitor the execution of the optimization plan by producing the required reports to implement corrective actions where needed.

**Qualifications/Requirements:**


Essential:

* Extensive experience (2\+ years) in Inventory management
* High school diploma or bachelor’s degree in: Engineering, Economics, Supply Chain, Data Science, Computer Science / Programming, Business/Economics
* Experience in leveraging handling large Data Sets, Data Acquisition, Transformation and Visualisation tools. (e.g. Microsoft Excel, Python, R, SQL, Tableau, PowerBI etc)
* Attention to detail, Lateral thinking \& Problem Solving
* Demonstrated conceptual, analytical skills \& logical thinking with statistical modelling / data analysis competency
* Ability to influence the decision\-making process by the use of data
* Good communicator
* Good business English


Desirable:

* Commercial/Customer facing experience
* Masters/PhD Degree in an associated field
* APICS Certification


At Johnson \& Johnson, we want every candidate to feel supported throughout the hiring process. Our goal is to make the experience clear, fair, and respectful of your time.

**Here’s what you can expect:**

* **Application review:** We’ll carefully review your CV to see how your skills and experience align with the role.
* **Getting to know you:** If there’s a good match, you’ll be invited to a short call with one of our recruitment team to understand more about you and answer any questions that you might have.
* **Staying informed:** We know waiting can be hard and processes can evolve; our recruitment team will keep you updated and make sure you know what to expect at each step.
* **Final steps:** For successful candidates, you will need to complete country\-specific checks before starting your new role. We will help guide you through these.


Finally, at the end of the process, we’ll invite you to share feedback in a short survey — your input helps us continue improving the experience for future candidates. Thank you for considering a career with Johnson \& Johnson. We’re excited to learn more about you and wish you the best of luck in the process! \#RPOEMEA


\#Li\-Hybrid

title: Talent Acquisition Trainee - Spanish & Italian Market
description:
Ecolab is looking for a **Talent Acquisition Trainee** for our **Europe Frontline Team** in Barcelona, supporting recruitment activities for the Spanish and Italian markets.

  

This is a great opportunity to start your career in international recruitment, working in a multicultural environment while gaining hands\-on experience in sourcing, screening and managing recruitment processes across Southern Europe.

**What's In It For You:**

* The opportunity to develop your career in Talent Acquisition within an international company.
* A dynamic, multicultural and collaborative work environment where you will work with colleagues from different countries.
* Hybrid working model: 3 days in the office and 2 days working from home
* Flexible working hours to support work\-life balance.
* The opportunity to contribute to meaningful work helping customers achieve clean water, safe food and healthy environments.

**What You Will Do:**

* Provide talent sourcing and recruiting support to your assigned market.
* Own the candidate search strategy for your assigned jobs.
* Prequalify, screen and assess candidates to ensure qualification matches capability to the organization.
* Partner with internal stakeholders to support a smooth interview and assessment process.
* Identify new sourcing channels, platforms and technology to help us continually improve.
* Proactively build high quality talent pipelines using social media, digital tools, databases and other research channels.
* Create compelling sourcing strategies and marketing materials, work on improving brand perception in target groups.
* Identify target industries, companies, user groups, professional associations or educational institutions to proactively create a pipeline of qualified candidates.

**Minimum Qualifications:**

* Ongoing or completed bachelor’s degree in HR, Business Administration, Psychology, or other related areas
* Fluency in English (written and spoken); Fluency in Spanish; Fluency in Italian; any other European language is a plus.
* Proficient in Microsoft Office
* Customer service focused, proactive and collaborative team player
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to build productive relationships
* Ability to multi\-task, effectively prioritize tasks and responsibilities, effective time management

**Location**: Barcelona (Sant Joan Despi Office)

*Ecolab’s commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion (DE\&I) reflects our longstanding value of working together to integrate diverse perspectives to challenge ourselves, reach our goals and do what’s right. Disability certificate will be an asset.*

  

\#li\-eu

 **Our Commitment to a Culture of Inclusion \& Belonging**  

Ecolab is committed to fair and equal treatment of associates and applicants and furthering the principles of Equal Opportunity to Employment. We will recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement based on individual qualifications and job performance in all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement. Ecolab will not discriminate against any associate or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin,citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, or disability.

title: Technical Due Diligence Expert - Biogas International (f/m/d)
description:
Technical Due Diligence Expert – Biogas International
Role Overview
The Technical Due Diligence Expert – Biogas International is responsible for performing comprehensive technical assessments of biogas and biomethane projects, with a strong focus on agricultural feedstock\-based plants across European markets. The role supports investment decisions, acquisitions, and project development by providing robust technical, operational, and risk analyses throughout the project lifecycle.
Key Responsibilities
Technical Due Diligence \& Project Assessment
Conduct technical due diligence for acquisitions, joint ventures, and greenfield biogas and biomethane projects.
Review and validate process design, plant layouts, mass and energy balances, and process flow diagrams.
Assess anaerobic digestion technologies, feedstock handling systems, biogas upgrading technologies, digestate treatment, and grid injection solutions.
Identify technical risks, mitigation measures, and value\-creation opportunities.

Engineering \& Plant Optimization
Evaluate feedstock availability, composition, and long\-term supply robustness.
Analyze plant performance, efficiency, reliability, and scalability, including repowering or expansion options.
Review compliance with applicable technical standards, safety requirements, and environmental regulations.
Support optimization of CAPEX and OPEX assumptions based on technical findings.

Transaction \& Investment Support
Provide technical input during investment committee processes, M\&A negotiations, and financial modeling.
Prepare clear and structured technical due diligence reports and executive summaries.
Respond to technical questions during transaction execution, including data room reviews and management meetings.
Support post\-acquisition integration and technical improvement roadmaps.

Stakeholder \& Supplier Management
Interface with technology providers, EPC contractors, operators, consultants, and regulatory bodies.
Validate supplier technical proposals, performance guarantees, and cost assumptions.
Collaborate closely with internal teams including development, finance, legal, and operations.

Market \& Regulatory Intelligence
Monitor developments in biogas and biomethane technologies, regulations, and sustainability schemes across Europe.
Contribute to internal best practices, technical guidelines, and knowledge sharing within the organization.

Required Qualifications
Education
Master’s degree in Energy Engineering, Renewable Energy, Environmental Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Agricultural Engineering, Agronomy, or a related discipline.

Professional Experience
Minimum of 3 years of experience in technical due diligence, project development, or engineering within the biogas or biomethane sector.
Proven experience with European biogas or biomethane projects, including agricultural feedstock\-based plants.
Background in consulting firms, energy companies, project developers, or investment organizations is highly desirable.

Technical Expertise
Strong knowledge of anaerobic digestion processes and biogas upgrading technologies.
Understanding of agricultural supply chains, feedstock logistics, and sustainability requirements.
Familiarity with project development phases, from feasibility studies to construction and operation.
Ability to translate technical assessments into commercial and financial implications.

Skills \& Competencies
Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
Ability to communicate complex technical topics clearly to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
Structured, detail\-oriented, and able to manage multiple projects in parallel.
Commercial mindset with a pragmatic and solution\-oriented approach.
Comfortable working in an international and fast\-paced environment.

Languages
Fluent in English (written and spoken).
Additional European languages such as Italian, Spanish, or Polish are considered an advantage.

Location
Flexible within Europe, with willingness to travel as required for project assessments and site visits.

What We Offer
A key role in the growth of an international biogas and biomethane platform.
Exposure to strategic investment and development projects across multiple European markets.
A dynamic, collaborative, and international working environment.
Opportunities for professional growth at the intersection of technology, sustainability, and energy investments.

Benefits:
At our company, we strive to create a culture of continuous learning, personal growth, and inter\- national community involvement. We're passionate about providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed, and we're confident that you'll love being part of our team!**Working** **Hours**  

Enjoy flexible working hours and the option to work up to 50% remotely – for the perfect work\-life balance.  

  

**Generous pension plan**  

We pay 60% of your pension contributions \- for a secure future. **Health comes first**  

100% continued payment of salary in the event of illness or accident and access to an exclusive mental health platform with five free sessions with a psychologist.  

  

**Parental leave**  

16 weeks maternity leave (100% continued pay) and 10 days paternity leave (100% continued pay) to spend valuable time with your family.  

  

**Support for your family**  

Up to 20% reimbursement of childcare costs (max. CHF 4,800 per year).  

  

**Exclusive discounts**  

Attractive discounts on petrol, insurance and mobile telephony with partner companies such as Shell, Avia, Sunrise and Swisscom.  

  

**Public transportation**  

No problem: with the SwissPass (max. CHF 190\) you can get from A to B at a reasonable price.  

  


  

  

  

*At Axpo Group, we are dedicated to fostering a culture of non\-discrimination, tolerance, and inclusion. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications regardless of race and ethnicity, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, as well as socioeconomic, cultural, and religious background. We are committed to ensuring a respectful and inclusive recruiting process and workplace for everyone.*

title: Sushi Chef de Partie - Porto Cervo
description:
Come Sushi Chef de Partie presso Zuma, avrai un ruolo fondamentale nella creazione dei nostri piatti signature, unendo tradizione e tocco contemporaneo. Per noi il sushi non è solo un piatto, è una vera e propria forma d’arte. Con la tua esperienza e dedizione, contribuirai a rafforzare la nostra reputazione nel creare esperienze gastronomiche indimenticabili per i nostri ospiti.  


Zuma è una collezione di ristoranti giapponesi contemporanei, ispirata al dinamico stile di ristorazione Izakaya. Questo ruolo sarà basato presso Zuma Porto Cervo. Situato lungo la splendida costa verde\-azzurra della Sardegna, il nostro ristorante offre un’esperienza stagionale davvero iconica in una delle destinazioni più prestigiose del Mediterraneo. Zuma Porto Cervo è orgogliosamente parte della Azumi Collection.  


Main duties  


Your key responsibilities will include:  


Maintain strong mise en place and organisation to ensure a smooth flow of dishes during service, delivering consistent quality and presentation.  


Prepare sushi dishes in line with Zuma recipes, ensuring precision in flavour, texture and presentation for every guest.  


Maintain high hygiene standards across storage and preparation areas, carrying out daily checks and keeping workstations clean and organised in line with Health \& Safety and Food Safety regulations.  


Lead the Maki section, supporting and guiding Chef de Parties while ensuring equipment is handled correctly and Zuma’s standards of quality and consistency are upheld.  


Benefits  


As part of our team, you’ll enjoy:  


World\-Class training, designed to inspire and educate  
Global opportunities, experience hospitality around the globe with our five incredible brands  
Family Meals are shared daily  
Rewards \+ Recognition, we value our team and celebrate your contributions to our success with meaningful rewards and recognition  
Dinning discount, enjoy exclusive discounts across the Azumi group, worldwide!  


Our Commitment to Inclusivity  
We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome all applicants. If you require any assistance to make the recruitment process more accessible, please let one of our Talent Acquisition team know.  


Ready to create some magic? Join us and bring your talent to a team that’s elevating contemporary dining on a global stage.

title: Digital Marketing Intern (Curriculare)
description:
No Relocation Assistance Offered  

Job Number \#171928 \- Roma, Lazio, Italy  

  

**Who We Are**  

  

Colgate\-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specialising in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name!  

  

Join Colgate\-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all.

 **The Opportunity**  

As a Marketing Intern, you will be part of our dynamic organization where you will gain hands\-on experience in one of our Category Marketing teams, gaining high\-visibility experience in the Digital FMCG (Fast\-Moving Consumer Goods) space. **Your key impact areas**:* Digital Product Launches: Assist in the end\-to\-end execution of new product launches, turning concepts into high\-performing online campaigns.

* Data\-Driven Insights: Analyze digital business data and consumer metrics to identify trends and optimize our online marketing strategies.

* E\-Commerce \& Project Management: Coordinate digital projects and contribute fresh ideas to enhance our e\-commerce platforms and user journeys.

* Social \& Web Presence: Support social media planning and website relaunches, gaining hands\-on experience in modern brand building.

* Market Intelligence: Stay ahead of the curve by researching digital FMCG trends and exploring how digital channels are reshaping consumer habits.

 **What We’re Looking For**  

You are a digital\-native "right hand" to help drive our online presence. You will be deeply involved in the technical execution of our digital strategy, managing our CRM ecosystem, and acting as a bridge between the company and our external agencies.### **Your Core Responsibilities**

* **Website \& Search:** Assist in managing website updates (CMS), monitoring SEO performance, and coordinating SEM/PPC campaigns to ensure brand consistency and performance.

* **CRM \& Lifecycle Marketing:** Take the lead on executing email marketing tracks, setting up automation workflows, and building acquisition campaigns that actually convert.

* **Agency Supervision:** Act as a primary point of contact for our digital agencies. You will help review their deliverables, ensure deadlines are met, and hold them accountable to our KPIs.

* **Data \& Optimization:** Track campaign performance and provide actionable insights. We don’t just want reports; we want suggestions on how to improve.

 **Required Qualifications**:* **Education**: Currently pursuing or recently graduated with a degree (Bachelor or Master) in Marketing, Business Administration, Advertising, or a related field.

* **Experience**: At least one previous internship or significant project experience in Marketing (ideally Consumer Goods), involving product launches or promotional campaigns.

* **Digital Savvy**: You understand the basics of SEO/SEM and are comfortable navigating CMS platforms (WordPress, Webflow, Shopify, etc.).

* **Analytical Mindset**: Comfortable working with numbers, spreadsheets, and marketing metrics to tell a story.

* **Soft Skills**: One of the most important skills: an entrepreneurial "self\-starter" attitude with the ability to collaborate effectively in a cross\-cultural environment.

* **High\-Level English:** You are **fluent in English** (written and spoken). You will be communicating with international partners and must be able to draft professional copy and lead meetings in English.

 **Location: Rome office** **VISA NOT SPONSORED** **Our Commitment to Inclusion**  

Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business.  

  

**Equal Opportunity Employer**  

Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law.  

  

Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.

ITALCERTIFER è leader di mercato in Italia delle certificazioni in ambito di Safety ferroviaria e Mass Transit ed opera in più di 30 Paesi nel mondo.
Per la gestione e l'ottimizzazione delle crescenti attività si ricerca **1 HR Generalist** **appartenete alle categorie protette ai sensi dell'Art. 1 della L. 68/99****.**

* Analizzare e monitorare le consistenze aziendali, il costo del lavoro e predisporre reportistica e forecast HR.

* Fornire supporto operativo su tematiche giuslavoristiche e nella gestione delle relazioni con i dipendenti.

 **Requisiti richiesti per la posizione ricercata:*** Laurea Magistrale e/o Vecchio Ordinamento in Giurisprudenza, Economia, Scienze Politiche, Consulenza del Lavoro o discipline affini.

* Contratto a tempo indeterminato del CCNL della Mobilità/ Area contrattuale Attività Ferroviarie.

Se hai voglia di fare parte del grande gruppo di Ferrovie dello Stato Italiane e sei in possesso dei requisiti richiesti, candidati **entro il giorno 20 marzo 2026**.  

Ti aspettiamo!

title: HR Generalist
description:
NtWK, azienda italiana operante nella ricerca e selezione del personale, si occupa di soddisfare le necessità dei clienti offrendo servizi personalizzati.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

Azienda operante nel settore alimentare ricerca, per la società holding, **un/una HR Generalist** da inserire all’interno del Dipartimento Risorse Umane.

  

La risorsa, a diretto riporto dell’HR Manager, si dedicherà alle seguenti attività:

  

– Amministrazione del personale


o Aggiornamento delle anagrafiche dei dipendenti


o Gestione dell’archivio cartaceo e digitale


o Elaborazione e quadratura delle presenze degli uffici e dei laboratori


o Aggiornamento dei KPI del personale (assenteismo, turnover, dettaglio delle ore notturne, domenicali, festive)


o Aggiornamento del budget del personale e calcolo del costo del personale


o Stesura della documentazione relativa all’intero ciclo del rapporto di lavoro (contratti di assunzione, proroghe, cessioni, cessazioni, distacchi…)


o Controllo dei cedolini, in particolare degli aspetti variabili della retribuzione (ore notturne, straordinari…)

  

– Sicurezza sul lavoro


o Gestione e calendarizzazione delle visite di idoneità pre\-assuntive e le scadenze periodiche


o Gestione e calendarizzazione dei corsi di formazione obbligatori ai sensi del TU Sicurezza nei luoghi di lavoro

  

– Formazione del personale


o Gradita esperienza nella gestione documentale di corsi formativi


o Gestione dei rapporti con enti accreditati dai fondi interprofessionali

  

– Selezione del personale


o Redazione delle job description


o Contatti con i candidati


o Screening dei curricula


o Interviste di selezione

**Requisiti:**
--------------

**PROFILO IDEALE**

* Preferibilmente laureato/a in materie umanistiche o economico/giuridiche;
* Buona conoscenza di Excel;
* Esperienza maturata nel ruolo di almeno un anno;
* Conoscenza base in ambito di amministrazione del personale e normativa giuslavoristica;
* Capacità di reportistica;
* Gradita conoscenza base di metodi di valutazione del personale;
* Gradita conoscenza della normativa e delle gestione della sicurezza nei luoghi di lavoro;
* Capacità di svolgere le proprie attività in autonomia;
* Attitudine al cambiamento e proattività nel proporre soluzione e nuove modalità operative.

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

Completano il profilo una personalità determinata, dinamica, flessibile e ottime capacità relazionali e di ascolto.

 **ULTERIORI INFORMAZIONI:**

**Zona di lavoro**: Ponte Milvio.

**Orario**: lunedì\-venerdì, dalle 9 alle 18 con 1h di pausa pranzo.

**Modalità**: In presenza.

title: Addetto/a al supporto operativo del Programma di qualificazione professionale
description:
IBIMI – Istituto per il BIM Italia \- associazione no\-profit impegnata nella promozione di metodi digitali nel settore delle costruzioni, iscritta negli elenchi del MIMIT ex legge 4/2013 e capitolo italiano di buildingSMART International, cerca una risorsa per curare l’operatività del Programma di Qualificazione Professionale.

Il Programma di Qualificazione Professionale mira ad offrire ai professionisti del settore percorsi formativi e di riconoscimento delle competenze in ambito BIM.

Il programma si divide in tre principali sottoprogrammi:

* Erogazione di corsi di formazione specialistici.
* Qualificazione di organismi di formazione partner per l'erogazione di corsi di formazione secondo gli standard dell’associazione.
* Centro di esame per il rilascio, mantenimento e rinnovo di certificazioni.

La risorsa verrà inserita in un contesto già strutturato, con processi e manuali operativi già definiti, ed entrerà a far parte di un team multidisciplinare. La risorsa dovrà occuparsi di:

* Gestire la vendita dei 3 prodotti: riconoscimento, formazione e certificazione.
* Organizzare, avviare e gestire l’erogazione di corsi e curare i rapporti con i maggiori stakeholder (utenti e docenti)
* Organizzare le sessioni di esame e curare i rapporti con i maggiori stakeholder (utenti, esaminatori, organismo di certificazione)
* Gestire l’iter di riconoscimento e rinnovo relativa agli Organismi di Formazione
* Gestione di alcuni aspetti del sito web e piattaforme digitali connesse al programma.
* Collaborazione con il team IT per migliorare e aggiornare le funzionalità dei portali e della piattaforma esame.

Cosa offriamo:

* Affiancamento iniziale;
* Lavoro a tempo pieno, inserimento dal livello 4 CCNL Studi professionali;
* Opportunità di crescita professionale ed incentivi al raggiungimento di obiettivi;
* Attività lavorativa principalmente da remoto con 1 incontro settimanale in ufficio nella sede di Roma centro (oltre alla presenza durante alcune fiere).
* Inserimento in una realtà dinamica ed in rapida crescita, riconosciuta a livello internazionale.

Requisiti del candidato ideale:

* Esperienza nel settore formazione e certificazione professionale
* Doti di precisione, organizzazione e problem solving
* Buone capacità comunicative e spirito di collaborazione.
* Dimestichezza con piattaforme moodle, wordpress, woocommerce, Microsoft Teams;
* PLUS: Buona conoscenza dell’inglese

Tempi e modalità di selezione:

L’inserimento è previsto il prima possibile. I colloqui di selezione si svolgeranno a partire dall’inizio del mese di aprile.

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno

Domande di preselezione:

* Abbiamo necessità di assumere in breve tempo, sei disponibile ad iniziare quanto prima?
* Hai la residenza a Roma e provincia?

Istruzione:

* Laurea triennale (Obbligatorio)

Esperienza:

* organizzatore corsi di formazione: 1 anno (Obbligatorio)

Lingua:

* Inglese (Obbligatorio)

Sede di lavoro: Ibrido/da remoto (00184 Roma)

description:

title: HR Business Partner, EU One PXT
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


\*\*This role is based fulltime in Jesi, relocation package can be offered if required\*\*  

  

\*\*Questo ruolo è basato a tempo pieno a Jesi, può essere offerto un pacchetto di trasferimento se necessario\*\*  

  

Creating a culture where all our people feel encouraged and supported is what makes Amazon a great place to work. As an HR Business Partner, you’ll drive a positive employee experience through your detailed grasp of HR programmes, processes, and tools, offering credible and clear HR advice to Amazon’s managers. From resolving complex HR queries with colleagues to perfecting new policies and programmes, you’ll be making a real difference in how Amazon operates.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Own and deliver projects and drive progress towards business goals  

Engage and coordinate with key stakeholders on data collection and policy implementation  

Resolve high\-level employee issues, including grievances, appeals and complex disciplinary issues  

Coach and develop HR colleagues and stakeholders across the business  

Where relevant, co\-ordinate with work councils and unions to drive positive employee relations  

Analyse internal dashboards to create reports on key metrics for leadership at your site  

  

A day in the life  

HR Business Partners get involved in a wide range of different projects and work with a mix of diverse stakeholders, all with one goal: to make Amazon a great place to work. You’ll be based in an Amazon fulfilment centre, delivery station or sortation centre and will frequently use data to inform key decisions.  

  

From one day to the next, you could be taking the lead on a range of different projects or supporting senior stakeholders with developing strategies and carrying out regular talent reviews. You’ll be taking a fresh look at our working culture and thinking of new ways to optimise them.  

  

You’ll also be supporting new colleagues to develop and learn new skills, so as well as shaping our working culture, you’ll be helping our colleagues grow. There will be plenty of opportunities for you to progress and shape your career at Amazon.  

  

About the team  

At Amazon, our HR department plays a vital role in looking after our people. Known as the People Experience and Technology Solutions (PXT) team, our priority is making the Amazon experience brilliant for our people, helping us remain one of the world’s most innovative, customer\-centric and nimble businesses.  

  

We operate proactively and take a holistic approach to people management. Our team is ‘Customer Obsessed’ and focuses on solving employee engagement issues quickly, ensuring nothing impacts our seamless employee and customer experience. Ultimately, it’s our job to insist on the highest standards and drive progress across Amazon’s strategy and goals.

**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* 1\. What are your yearly salary expectations?
* 2\. Are you currently located in, or willing to relocate to, the location of this position?
* 3\. Are you willing and able to be onsite Monday through Friday (5 days a week) for this role?
* 1\. What are your monthly gross salary expectations?

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Do you have experience with MS Office suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) in a professional environment?

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Représentant - Comptabilité salaires et récompenses - Temporaire
description:
**Appartenir à Air Canada, c’est appartenir à un symbole canadien, Air Canada récemment élue meilleur transporteur aérien en Amérique du Nord. Faites décoller votre carrière en vous joignant à notre équipe novatrice et diversifiée à l’avant\-garde du transport aérien de passagers.**


Relevant du partenaire – Rémunération hors Canada, la personne qui occupe ce poste hybride apportera son soutien au service des Ressources humaines – Réseau international à l’échelle mondiale. Elle sera chargée de l’administration du système de gestion de l’assiduité (Workforce Manager d’ADP) et agira en tant que représentant au recrutement et à la rémunération pour le réseau international. Elle dirigera les relations avec les parties prenantes internes qui utilisent Workforce Manager comme outil de gestion de l’assiduité, et sera responsable de l’administration et du bon fonctionnement du système. Elle sera également responsable des dossiers de recrutement et soutiendra les programmes de rémunération pour le réseau international.


Ce poste peut être basé à Rome, Francfort, Londres, Paris, en Espagne ou dans d'autres pays européens.

**Responsabilités :**


Gestion de l’assiduité

* Assumer la responsabilité de la gestion quotidienne de tous les problèmes liés au système de gestion de l’assiduité dans Workforce Manager (WFM) sur les marchés internationaux, y compris le soutien à la mise en œuvre, le dépannage et la documentation appropriée, ainsi que la communication avec les différentes parties prenantes (telles que l’équipe des Ressources humaines – Réseau international, ADP et les utilisateurs).
* Contribuer à la mise en œuvre de WFM dans de nouveaux pays en fournissant des fichiers démographiques et des structures commerciales, participer aux tests, prendre part à la stratégie de communication, et former les employés et les gestionnaires.
* Agir à titre de principale personne\-ressource pour les gestionnaires et les employés en cas de problèmes liés au système WFM.
* Remplir le formulaire de champ de saisie d’ADP pour les nouveaux employés et les employés promus.
* Ajuster la configuration du système WFM en fonction des mises à jour et des modifications apportées au fichier démographique (p. ex., ensembles organisationnels et requêtes Hyperfind).
* Enquêter sur les erreurs quotidiennes signalées et les analyser afin de mettre en œuvre les mesures nécessaires.
* Assumer la responsabilité de tous les tickets de demande de service auprès d’ADP et assurer un suivi afin de garantir la configuration et le rendement exacts du système WFM.
* Exécuter l’intégration mensuelle des données pour tous les registres de paie (ADP) avec transfert automatisé des données vers le registre de paie, en coordination avec les équipes des Ressources humaines – Réseau international et de la Comptabilité salaires.
* Soutenir les clients lors des mises à jour (p. ex., charges à payer).
* Créer une structure hiérarchique harmonisée.
* Créer des rapports mensuels et annuels en fonction des besoins locaux, les transcrire dans PowerPoint et présenter les résultats à l’équipe des Ressources humaines – Réseau international et aux clients internes.
* Apporter un soutien à d’autres tâches selon les besoins.


Recrutement

* Assumer la responsabilité du suivi des dossiers de recrutement, notamment auprès de l’équipe des Ressources humaines – Réseau international.
* Créer des mises à jour hebdomadaires pour les clients internes en ce qui a trait à leurs dossiers de recrutement.
* Mettre à jour le registre de recrutement et eDotation selon les besoins.
* Collaborer avec le recruteur – Talents dans le cadre des dossiers.


Rémunération

* Soutenir les programmes annuels d’Air Canada destinés aux employés internationaux, y compris (mais sans s’y limiter) le programme salarial annuel.
* Collaborer avec la Comptabilité salaires pour la mise en œuvre de tous les programmes de rémunération pour le réseau international.
* Participer aux analyses comparatives et aux enquêtes salariales de la Rémunération. Recueillir des données d’études de marché à l’appui de diverses initiatives de la Rémunération.
* Effectuer une recherche statistique approfondie, notamment des études de marché à l’appui de diverses initiatives de la Rémunération.
* Bâtir des relations avec les services internes.
* Participer à l’élaboration et à la mise à jour des politiques relevant de la Rémunération hors Canada (p. ex., conditions de travail, échelles salariales), notamment au moyen de sondages, de recherches, d’analyses, de recommandations et d’interprétations.
* Tenir à jour ses connaissances de l’industrie, de l’évolution législative et des meilleures pratiques.
* Réviser et mettre à jour les procédures et les directives en matière de rémunération.
* S’acquitter de toutes ses autres tâches, le cas échéant.

**Qualifications**

* Diplôme collégial ou universitaire dans une discipline liée aux ressources humaines, un atout.
* Titre pertinent en ressources humaines, un atout.
* Connaissance du logiciel Workforce Manager (WFM) d’ADP, un atout.
* Au moins deux ans d’expérience en administration de systèmes de gestion de l’assiduité, en recrutement ou en rémunération.
* Solides compétences en informatique relatives à la suite Office de Microsoft (Word, Outlook, PowerPoint et Excel); capacité à traiter les données et à produire la correspondance, les rapports et d’autres documents.
* Habileté à travailler sous pression avec rapidité et précision ainsi qu’à établir les priorités, tout en maintenant un service clientèle de qualité supérieure.
* Habiletés manifestes en matière de service clientèle et sens aigu de la diplomatie
* Habileté à travailler dans un environnement axé sur le travail d’équipe.
* Excellentes habiletés pour les relations interpersonnelles et la communication.
* Grande habileté à analyser et à exploiter les données.
* Capacité d’apprendre rapidement à utiliser de nouveaux systèmes.
* Capacité à assumer la responsabilité de son domaine de compétence.
* Sens marqué de l’organisation.

**Conditions d’emploi :**


Les candidats doivent être admissibles à travailler dans le pays concerné au moment où une offre d’emploi est présentée et sont responsables de l’obtention des permis de travail, visas ou autres autorisations nécessaires. La preuve de l’admissibilité doit être fournie avant la date de début.

**Exigences Linguistiques**


Maîtrise de l’anglais. Maîtrise du français, de l’allemand, de l’espagnol et de l'italien, un atout.

**Diversité et inclusion**


Air Canada est résolument engagée en faveur de la diversité et de l’inclusion et vise à créer un milieu de travail sain, accessible et gratifiant qui met en valeur la contribution unique de nos employés au succès de notre entreprise.


En tant qu'employeur qui garantit l'égalité d'accès à l'emploi, nous encourageons les candidatures les plus diverses afin de pouvoir nous doter d’un effectif varié et représentatif de nos clients et des communautés où nous vivons et offrons nos services.

**Air Canada remercie tous les candidats de leur intérêt, mais seules les personnes sélectionnées pour une entrevue seront contactées.**
company_description: Air Canada is among the 20 largest airlines globally. Its corporate headquarters are located in Montreal.

title: Payroll and Rewards Representative - Temporary
description:
**Being part of Air Canada is to become part of an iconic Canadian symbol, recently ranked the best Airline in North America. Let your career take flight by joining our diverse and vibrant team at the leading edge of passenger aviation.**


Reporting to the International Compensation Partner, this hybrid position will support the International Human Resources department globally and will be responsible for the administration of the time \& attendance system (ADP Workforce Manager) and will act as a representative for international recruitment and compensation. The selected individual will lead the relationships with internal stakeholders who use Workforce Manager as their time and attendance tool and will be responsible for the administration and proper functioning of the system. In addition, the individual will lead the recruitment files and support the compensation programs for the international portfolio.


This position can be located in Rome, Frankfurt, London, Paris, Spain and other European countries.

**Responsibilities:**


Time and Attendance

* Responsible for the day\-to\-day handling of all time \& attendance system issues in Workforce Manager (WFM) in international markets including implementation support, trouble shooting and proper documentation, and communication with the different stakeholders (such as the International HR team, ADP, and users).
* Assist with the implementations of WFM for new countries by delivering demographic files and business structures, assist with the testing, be part of the communication strategy, and train employees and managers.
* Be the main contact for managers and employees for any WFM related issues.
* Complete the ADP input field form for new hires and promotions.
* Adjust the WFM set\-up upon updates and changes in the demographic file (e.g. org sets, and hyperfinds).
* Investigate and analyze the reported daily errors and process necessary actions.
* Responsible for all Service request tickets with ADP and follow\-up to secure accurate set\-up and performance of WFM.
* Process monthly data integration run for all ADP payrolls with automated data transfer to payroll in coordination with international HR team and payroll.
* Support clients with updates (e.g. accruals, etc.).
* Create harmonized reporting structure.
* Create monthly and annual reports based on local needs, transcript on PowerPoint and present the results to the international HR team and internal clients.
* Support other duties as required.


Recruitment

* Responsible for tracking and follow\-up with the international HR team on recruitment files.
* Create weekly updates for internal clients on their recruitment files.
* Update the recruitment log and eStaffs as appropriate.
* Collaborate with the talent recruiter for files.


Compensation

* Support Air Canada’s annual programs for international employees including (but not specific to) the Annual Salary Program.
* Collaborate with payroll for the delivery for all international compensation programs.
* Participate in Compensation benchmarking and salary surveys. Collect market survey data in support of various compensation initiatives.
* Perform in\-depth statistical research including market surveys in support of Compensation initiatives.
* Build relationships with internal departments.
* Participate in the development and/or updating of international Compensation\-owned policies (e.g. Employment Conditions and Salary Scales) including surveys, research, analysis, recommendations, and interpretation.
* Maintain awareness and knowledge of industry, legislative developments, and best practices.
* Review and update procedures, and compensation guidelines.
* Perform all other tasks as required.

**Qualifications**

* College or University degree in Human Resources related disciplines, an asset.
* Relevant HR designation, an asset.
* Knowledge of the ADP Workforce Manager (WFM), an asset.
* Minimum two (2\) years experience in administrating time \& attendance systems and/or recruitment and/or compensation.
* Proficient computer skills using Microsoft Office: Word, Outlook, PowerPoint and Excel and ability to manipulate date and produce correspondence, reports, and other documents.
* Ability to work under pressure with speed and accuracy and establish priorities while maintaining a high customer service level.
* Demonstrated customer\-service orientation and high degree of diplomacy.
* Ability to work in a team\-oriented environment.
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
* Strong ability to analyze and play with data.
* Ability to quickly learn new systems.
* Ability to take ownership for the area of responsibility.
* Strong organizational skills.

**Conditions of Employment:**


Candidates must be eligible to work in the country of interest at the time any offer of employment is made and are responsible for obtaining any required work permits, visas, or other authorizations necessary for employment. Prior to their start date, candidates will also need to provide proof of their eligibility to work in the country of interest.

**Linguistic Requirements**


Based on equal (Fluent in English, French, German, Spanish and Italian an asset)

**Diversity and Inclusion**


Air Canada is strongly committed to Diversity and Inclusion and aims to create a healthy, accessible and rewarding work environment which highlights employees’ unique contributions to our company’s success.


As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from all to help us build a diverse workforce which reflects the diversity of our customers, and communities, in which we live and serve.

**Air Canada thanks all candidates for their interest; however only those selected to continue in the process will be contacted**

title: HR Administration Specialist
description:
Il gruppo LDC vanta una magnifica collezione di hotel di lusso, relais, ristoranti e location per eventi, dislocati tra Taiwan e l’Italia. LDC è l’acronimo di “Luxury, Dreams and Culture”: tre valori fondamentali che ispirano profondamente il nostro concetto di ospitalità.

Ricerchiamo per il nostro Headquarter, situato presso l'A.Roma Lifestyle Hotel, la seguente figura professionale:

**HR Administration Specialist** 

Garantendo accuratezza, conformità normativa e supporto operativo ai dipendenti e al management, il ruolo richiede una forte attenzione al dettaglio, capacità organizzative e padronanza dei principali sistemi HR, con particolare riferimento alla suite Zucchetti.

**Responsabilità principali**

* Gestione completa del ciclo amministrativo del personale: assunzioni, proroghe, trasformazioni, cessazioni.
* Predisposizione e aggiornamento della documentazione contrattuale e delle comunicazioni obbligatorie.
* Raccolta, verifica e inserimento delle presenze, assenze, ferie, malattie e permessi ai fini dell’elaborazione paghe.
* Interfaccia operativa con il payroll provider e verifica dei cedolini.
* Gestione delle indennità e benefit
* Monitoraggio delle scadenze contrattuali
* Supporto ai dipendenti per richieste amministrative, policy e procedure interne.
* Produzione di reportistica HR (turnover, assenteismo, costi del personale).
* Supporto ai processi di onboarding e offboarding.

**Competenze tecniche richieste**

* Ottima conoscenza della normativa del lavoro, la conoscenza del CCNL turismo Federalberghi sarà considerato un plus.
* **Conoscenza operativa del software Zucchetti Paghe Infinity.**
* **Esperienza nell’utilizzo del Workflow Zucchetti** per gestione presenze, richieste, approvazioni e processi HR.
* Ottima padronanza di Excel e del pacchetto Office

**Requisiti**

* Diploma o Laurea in discipline economiche, giuridiche o risorse umane.
* Minimi due anni di esperienza in ruoli di amministrazione del personale o HR Operations.
* Precisione, riservatezza e affidabilità nella gestione di dati sensibili.
* Capacità di lavorare per scadenze e gestire più attività contemporaneamente.
* Ottime capacità comunicative e orientamento al servizio.

**Soft Skills**

* Organizzazione e gestione delle priorità.
* Problem solving operativo.
* Attitudine collaborativa e capacità di interfacciarsi con diversi stakeholder.
* Flessibilità e adattabilità in contesti dinamici.
* Orientamento alla qualità e alla compliance

Si offre contratto a tempo determinato con possibilità di stabilizzazione

Retribuzione: commisurata alla esperienza

title: Addetta alla gestione del personale
Stage Risorse Umane
description:
Airfire Spa è una realtà consolidata operante nel settore Antincendio ed in forte espansione nel settore edile, orientata all’eccellenza organizzativa e allo sviluppo del capitale umano.

In un’ottica di potenziamento della Direzione Risorse Umane, siamo alla ricerca di una risorsa da inserire in stage a supporto delle attività di funzione.

**Principali responsabilità**

La risorsa, inserita all’interno dell’Ufficio Risorse Umane e a diretto riporto dell’HR Manager, sarà coinvolta nelle seguenti attività:

* Supporto nella gestione amministrativa del personale (predisposizione documentazione, archiviazione, aggiornamento anagrafiche),
* Aggiornamento e monitoraggio dei database HR e predisposizione di reportistica tramite portale Zucchetti,
* Supporto operativo nei processi di **ricerca e selezione del personale** (pubblicazione annunci, screening curricula, organizzazione colloqui),
* Affiancamento nella conduzione di colloqui conoscitivi e gestione del processo di selezione,
* Supporto alle attività di onboarding e induction dei nuovi inserimenti,
* Collaborazione a progetti trasversali in ambito formazione e sviluppo organizzativo,

**Requisiti richiesti**

* Laurea triennale o magistrale (conseguita o in corso) in , Economia o discipline affini,
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Microsoft Office, in particolare Excel,
* Ottime capacità comunicative e relazionali,
* Precisione, affidabilità e riservatezza nella gestione delle informazioni,
* Forte interesse per l’ambito delle Risorse Umane,

**Completano il profilo**

* Attitudine al lavoro in team,
* Proattività e orientamento al risultato,
* Buone capacità organizzative e di gestione delle priorità,

**Cosa offriamo**

* Percorso formativo strutturato e affiancamento continuo,
* Inserimento in un contesto professionale dinamico e orientato alla crescita,
* Opportunità di acquisire competenze operative nei principali processi HR,
* Rimborso spese di €1000,00 netti al mese,
* Stage della durata di 6 mesi, full time dalle ore 09:00 alle ore 18:00,
* Possibilità concreta di rimanere in azienda alla fine del percorso di stage.

*Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03\.*

*La selezione è aperta a tutte le persone, senza distinzione di genere, identità o espressione di genere, orientamento sessuale, età, origine etnica, disabilità, stato di gravidanza, credo religioso o appartenenza politica, nel rispetto del D.lgs. 198/2006 e dei D.lgs. 215/2003 e 216/2003\.*

*L’annuncio è redatto nel rispetto dei principi di pari opportunità e inclusività, in conformità alla normativa vigente.*

*Crediamo nel valore della diversità e ci impegniamo a costruire un ambiente di lavoro equo, accogliente e inclusivo, dove ogni persona possa esprimere il proprio potenziale e sentirsi rispettata e valorizzata.*

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno, Tempo determinato

Retribuzione: €900,00 \- €1\.000,00 al mese

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: HR Assistant L.68/99 - Categoria Protetta Part Time
description:
***Entra nel nostro mondo.***

***Trasforma insieme a noi.***

  

  

Nati nel 2003, abbiamo raccolto e valorizzato l’esperienza storica della consulenza e abbiamo aggiunto due ingredienti chiave: l’innovazione e la digitalizzazione.  



Grazie a questo percorso siamo oggi oltre 4\.500 professionisti e professioniste, presenti in 13 Paesi, con oltre 4\.500 progetti alle spalle e le conoscenze più all’avanguardia nell'ambito della Digital Transformation, Data Science, Cybersecurity, Industry 4\.0, IOT e di tutte le Disruptive Technologies che mettiamo a servizio di ogni settore di mercato.


Aiutiamo i nostri clienti a fare la differenza creando qualità su larga scala attraverso una formula che opera su tre leve: Valore, Persone e Tecnologia.  



Crediamo nel valore dell’eccellenza, che è la bussola su cui orientiamo il nostro agire, e adottiamo un approccio etico e leale nei confronti di quante e quanti scelgono di lavorare con noi, promuovendo un ambiente in cui le persone possano crescere insieme, grazie alla contaminazione tra competenze diverse.  



**HR Assistant L.68/99 – Categoria Protetta Part Time**

**Quale sarà la tua sfida?**

Per la nostra sede di Bologna siamo alla ricerca di una risorsa qualificata che porti passione e determinazione all'interno del nostro team HR. La nuova risorsa supporterà l’HRBP di riferimento in attività di Recruiting.  



La risorsa si occuperà delle seguenti attività:


* Gestione dell’intero processo di ricerca e selezione (es. stesura e pubblicazione annunci, screening CV, conduzione colloqui, proposta di inserimento, ecc.);
* Gestione e aggiornamento del database aziendale;
* Produzione e gestione di reportistica;
* Promozione e pianificazione di eventi mirati in collaborazione con le università (career day, job fair, ecc.);
Coinvolgimento in progetti ad hoc.  
* 

**Sarai tu ad entrare nel nostro team?**

* Almeno 1/2 anni di esperienza maturata (anche in stage) in attività di ricerca e selezione in società di Head Hunting e/o società di selezione del personale;
* Ottima dimestichezza con l’utilizzo di LinkedIn Recruiter;
* Eccellente conoscenza del pacchetto MS Office;
* Buona conoscenza della lingua Inglese;
* Ottime doti relazionali e comunicative;
* Affidabilità, flessibilità, puntualità e precisione;
Proattività, problem\-solving e multitasking.  
* 

Sede di lavoro: Roma.  



**Perché BIP?**

***People at the center of our culture***

Fiducia e collaborazione, imprenditorialità e coraggio, meritocrazia e sviluppo guidano la crescita delle nostre persone.


Lavorare in BIP è un'esperienza sfidante, in cui il merito paga e l'innovazione avviene attraverso idee coraggiose, collaborazione e un rapporto di fiducia con i nostri clienti.  



***Challenging yourself***  

Avrai la possibilità di metterti alla prova in contesti progettuali diversi in termini di ambito, natura delle attività e stakeholders coinvolti. Vedrai valorizzato il tuo spirito di iniziativa, la tua passione, l’autonomia e la capacità di assumerti responsabilità e metterti in gioco.  



***Unlocking your potential***  

Potrai partecipare a \+ di 300 corsi formativi all’avanguardia per accrescere le tue competenze su tecnologie e temi di business emergenti.  



***Work\-life Integration***  

Troverai una policy che supporta il lavoro AGILE fino al 100% del proprio tempo e favorisce il work\-life integration.  



***Diversity, Equity and Inclusion***

Valorizziamo l’unicità e ci impegniamo a garantire che tutte le nostre persone abbiano pari opportunità di dare un contributo ed esprimere al massimo il potenziale nell'ambiente di lavoro.


Il nostro approccio alla diversità e all’inclusione si fonda sui principi di etica e integrità e rappresenta il fattore determinante per lo sviluppo di orizzonti, di crescita personale e aziendale, più ampi.


Promuoviamo l’inserimento e l’integrazione lavorativa delle persone appartenenti alle categorie protette – in base a quanto disciplinato dalla legge 68/99  



  

***Next Steps***

Una volta ricevuto il tuo cv ci prenderemo del tempo per valutarlo attentamente.


Se c’è un match con questa o con altre posizioni aperte all’interno del Gruppo, ti contatteremo per iniziare la nostra conoscenza reciproca.


L’iter di selezione comprende generalmente un colloquio con HR, un colloquio tecnico con un line manager ed un eventuale colloquio con un Partner.


L’ordine e il numero delle interviste potrebbero variare a seconda delle esigenze di recruiting.


In alternativa, saremo felici di conservare il tuo CV nel nostro database per poterlo rivalutare per altre opportunità future.


Se dovessi identificare ulteriori posizioni di tuo interesse ed in linea con il tuo profilo, aspettiamo la tua candidatura per poterla valutare nuovamente.


Il presente annuncio si rivolge ai candidati di ambo i sessi (L. 903/77\).

La risorsa selezionata si occuperà della gestione amministrativa e documentale del personale, supportando le attività di selezione, archiviazione e collaborazione con agenzie interinali.

Offerta

title: Addetto/a Selezione e Formazione Operativa – Settore commerciale
description:
Azienda commerciale attiva nel settore del largo consumo inserisce una risorsa nell’area risorse umane con attività operative legate alla gestione delle candidature, all’organizzazione dei colloqui e al supporto alla formazione iniziale delle nuove risorse.



Il ruolo si svolge in collaborazione con i responsabili interni e con il team dedicato all’inserimento, con l’obiettivo di mantenere ordinato il flusso di selezione e accompagnare i nuovi ingressi nelle prime fasi operative.



Attività principali


* Screening dei CV e prima valutazione dei profili in base ai requisiti richiesti
* Contatto dei candidati e fissaggio dei colloqui (calendario, conferme, aggiornamenti)
* Supporto nella gestione dei colloqui: convocazioni, raccolta informazioni e predisposizione documenti interni
* Aggiornamento dei dati candidatura su file e strumenti aziendali (Excel, posta elettronica, gestionale)
* Affiancamento al team interno per la formazione operativa di ingresso: organizzazione sessioni, materiali, tracciamento presenze e feedback
* Supporto amministrativo di base legato all’inserimento (raccolta documenti e check\-list)


Requisiti richiesti


* Diploma di scuola superiore (titolo equivalente)
* Buone capacità comunicative e approccio ordinato nella gestione dei contatti
* Precisione, riservatezza e capacità di lavorare su più attività
* Utilizzo base di strumenti digitali (Excel, posta elettronica, strumenti di calendario)
* Interesse per attività di selezione e inserimento del personale


Offerta e benefit


* Contratto a tempo determinato di 3 mesi
* Orario full time dal lunedì al venerdì
* Inserimento con affiancamento e formazione interna sul metodo di lavoro


Sede di lavoro: Frosinone

title: Commerciale Servizi HR (divisione Finance)
description:
Ti piacerebbe far parte di un’azienda che sappia coltivare il tuo potenziale e che investa sul tuo sviluppo?
  
  

Tu sei il talento, noi l'opportunità!
  
  

Chi siamo?
  
  

Randstad.
  
Leading Company nel settore HR.
  
Siamo una multinazionale olandese presente in 39 paesi, con oltre 280 filiali su tutto il territorio a livello nazionale.
  
  

**Commerciale Servizi HR (divisione Finance):**  

Randstad Finance è la divisione specializzata nella ricerca e selezione di professionisti nei settori Finance \& Administration, Banking e Insurance.
  
  

In un’ottica di potenziamento della nostra rete commerciale specializzata, cerchiamo un/una Business Developer / Commerciale servizi HR per la nostra divisione Finance, a presidio del territorio di Roma e Provincia.
  
  

**Il Tuo Ruolo:** Sviluppo Business e Consulenza
  
  

Sarai il volto di Randstad sul territorio di Roma e Provincia (con possibilità di estendere il territorio di riferimento alla regione Lazio e, successivamente, al Centro Sud Italia). Il tuo obiettivo principale sarà lo sviluppo del mercato attraverso la vendita di servizi HR complessi, agendo come un vero partner strategico per le aziende clienti.
  
  

**Le tue principali responsabilità e attività:**  

**Sviluppo Commerciale:** Acquisizione di nuovi clienti, gestione e consolidamento del portafoglio esistente nel settore Finance (Banking, Insurance, F\&A).
  
  

**Consulenza di Business:** Analisi del mercato locale e dei bisogni aziendali per proporre soluzioni di somministrazione e permanent placement che favoriscano la crescita dei clienti.
  
  

**Strategia e Negoziazione:** Pianificazione commerciale proattiva, presentazione di offerte economiche e negoziazione dei contratti.
  
  

**Account Management:** Conoscenza profonda del database e segmentazione dei clienti, con focus su attività di cross\-selling e raggiungimento dei budget prefissati.
  
  

Chi stiamo cercando?
  
  

Siamo alla ricerca di una persona intraprendente, che sappia parlare il linguaggio del business e che ami la sfida commerciale in un contesto tecnico e strutturato.
  
  

**Formazione:** Laurea o Diploma (un background economico o giuridico sarà considerato un plus).
  
  

**Esperienza:** Esperienza pregressa nella vendita di servizi B2B preferibilmente nel settore HR.
  
  

**Mindset:** Forte orientamento ai risultati, doti negoziali, spirito proattivo e curiosità verso il settore finanziario.
  
  

**Competenze:** Capacità di pianificazione e gestione delle priorità per il raggiungimento degli obiettivi di fatturato.
  
  

Cosa offriamo
  
  

**Inquadramento:** Contratto commisurato all'esperienza, 14 mensilità e Ticket Restaurant.
  
  

**Premialità:** Sistema di bonus al raggiungimento degli obiettivi e piano di welfare aziendale.
  
  

**Crescita:** Percorso di training specialistico per diventare un punto di riferimento commerciale nel settore Finance.
  
  

**Flessibilità:** Modalità di lavoro ibrida (previo periodo di induction) per un reale equilibrio vita\-lavoro.
  
  

Perché scegliere Randstad
  
  

Perché entrerai in un ambiente dinamico dove la competenza tecnica incontra la sfida commerciale. Avrai l'autonomia per sviluppare il business e diventare un vero \#HumanDiProfessione.
  
  

La ricerca è rivolta a candidati ambosessi (L.903/77\). Ti invitiamo a consultare l'informativa sulla privacy su randstad.it/privacy, ai sensi dell’art. 13 del Regolamento UE 2016/679\.

title: Cabin Crew Application Rome Fiumicino 24 March 2026 - Evergreen
description:
**Cabin Crew Recruitment Day \- Rome Fiumicino \- 24 March 2026**

  



Date and time: 24 March 2026 09:00 am sharp  

Location: to be shared after prescreening

  



Have you ever dreamed of a unique place of work while being on the go and meeting new people? We have good news for you: our Cabin Crew life is just like that!



We are continuously searching for positive, energetic and motivated candidates for flight attendant/cabin crew positions to join our award\-winning team.

  


**JOIN OUR RECRUITMENT DAYS!**



We are constantly recruiting – browse through the upcoming events nearest to you.

  


**RECRUITMENT DAY good to knows:**



Read the requirements listed below as your compliance will be checked during the recruitment event.



Arrive sharply on time and be prepared that the process might take until late afternoon.



Bring your updated English CV with ID photo.



Please be advised that you may join this recruitment event regardless of the base you are applying for in Wizz Air Malta. If you want to join any other base in the network, please attend a recruitment event locally.

  


**Working as a Wizz Air flight attendant, you will:**


* Focus on both safety and customer care aspects
* Perform on board and ground duties in a way to comply with the Company’s policies
* Manage in\-flight sales and cash handling
* Welcome passengers with a smile and help them during the flight
* Act in accordance with our values: dedication, inclusivity, positivity, integrity
* Be part of a new family of more than 5,000 aviation enthusiasts

  


**Requirements:**


* You are aged 18 or over
* You have an arm reach of 210 cm while standing on tiptoes
* You have high school degree
* You are fluent – written and spoken – in English
* You have a valid passport without limitations (minimum 6 months) and the right to work in the country you are applying for
* You are able to swim
* You live or are ready to move within 60 minutes of your chosen base and prepared to make this journey upon work schedule
* You don’t have tattoos and/or piercing on parts of the body that are visible when wearing uniform
* You are an enthusiastic person who likes working with people
* Please note that if you wear glasses or contact lenses and your prescription is above \+4 or below \-4, you are unlikely to pass the mandatory medical examination

  


**What can we offer:**

  


**Perks and benefits:** competitive salary, social security \& work insurance, complete and free Cabin Crew training, free tickets based on experience and employee discounted tickets, paid holiday


**Career development opportunities:** at one of the fastest growing airlines


**Work\-life balance:** no layovers \- opportunity to lead normal life in your home town, flexible roster, possibility to swap duties/working days vs. OFF days


**Wizz Air Culture:**


* **SPORT EVENTS:** Wizz Air Half Marathons and Running events
* **BASE EVENTS:** bowling, karaoke, paintball, hot chocolate/fruit day etc.
* **WORK \& TRAVEL:** opportunity to work at different Wizz Air bases upon need/request
* **WORK ON SPECIAL/VIP FLIGHTS:** open flights, anniversary events
*

title: Business Operations Assistant and Events coordinator
description:
DEADLINE FOR APPLICATIONS
23 March 2026\-23:59\-GMT\+01:00 Central European Time (Rome)*WFP celebrates and embraces diversity. It is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all its employees and encourages qualified candidates to apply irrespective of race, colour, national origin, ethnic or social background, genetic information, gender, gender identity and/or expression, sexual orientation, religion or belief, HIV status or disability.*  

ABOUT WFP  

  

The World Food Programme is the world’s largest humanitarian organization saving lives in emergencies and using food assistance to build a pathway to peace, stability and prosperity, for people recovering from conflict, disasters and the impact of climate change.  

At WFP, people are at the heart of everything we do and the vision of the future WFP workforce is one of diverse, committed, skilled, and high performing teams, selected on merit, operating in a healthy and inclusive work environment, living WFP's values (Integrity, Collaboration, Commitment, Humanity, and Inclusion) and working with partners to save and change the lives of those WFP serves.  

  

To learn more about WFP, visit our website: https://www.wfp.org
WHY JOIN WFP?* WFP is a 2020 Nobel Peace Prize Laureate.
* WFP offers a highly inclusive, diverse, and multicultural working environment.
* WFP invests in the personal \& professional development of its employees through a range of training, accreditation, coaching, mentorship, and other programs as well as through internal mobility opportunities.
* A career path in WFP provides an exciting opportunity to work across the various country, regional and global offices around the world, and with passionate colleagues who work tirelessly to ensure that effective humanitarian assistance reaches millions of people across the globe.
* We offer an attractive compensation package (please refer to the Terms and Conditions section of this vacancy announcement).


JOB TITLE: Business operations assistant and Events coordinator
UNIT/DIVISION: Private Partnerships Division (PSP)
TEAM: Global Philanthropy
DUTY STATION: Rome HQ
CONTRACT TYPE: CST REG
DURATION: 11 months
WORK SCHEDULE: Full\-time
LINE MANAGER/SUPERVISOR: Deputy Director
ORGANIZATIONAL CONTEXT
The United Nations World Food Programme is the world's largest humanitarian agency fighting hunger worldwide, directly assisting 152 million people in 2023\. The needs are still immense, and the 2025 SOFI report estimates that between 638 and 720 million people, corresponding to 7\.8 and 8\.8 percent of the global population, respectively, faced hunger in 2024\. WFP’s mission is to help the world achieve Zero Hunger in our lifetime. Each year, WFP works globally as an emergency responder, one that is often the first on the scene in the most difficult environments. WFP also works with partners to strengthen the resilience of communities most vulnerable to hunger and malnutrition through long\-term development programmes aimed at boosting food security, local economies, education, employment and gender equality.
To contribute to a world with Zero Hunger, WFP is investing in and growing private sector partnerships to reach more people. The Private Sector Partnerships Division (PSP) is responsible for engaging the private sector (corporations, individuals, foundations, philanthropy) through high\-impact partnerships in support of WFP's strategic priorities, and through individual giving to build and retain support from the general public worldwide. Partnerships can be fundraising or/ and technical in nature, with some bringing purely financial resources to the organization, whereas other bringing in\-kind support, in the form of expertise and assets. PSP mobilizes funding, technical assistance, innovative solutions, visibility and awareness to help WFP fulfil its mission and operations. PSP also provides guidance to WFP Regional Bureaus (RBs), Country Offices (COs) and HQ Technical Units on partnership development, best practices, as well as compliance with rules and regulations for engaging with the private sector. Joining PSP is an exciting opportunity for experienced professionals to co\-develop and implement our new private partnerships and fundraising strategy.
PURPOSE OF THE ASSIGNMENT
Reporting to the Division Deputy Director overseeing the Global Philanthropy workstream, the Business operations assistant and Events coordinator will provide operational support to ensure professional, fluid and effective follow\-up of partnerships and engagements, including agreements, event logistics and meetings. The role will also manage events end\-to\-end related to WFP ‘s engagement and cultivation of High\-Net\-Worth Individuals and Family Offices. These will include: bilateral meetings, fire\-side chats, intimate gatherings, panels, dinners and high\-level multi\-stakeholder events. Our ideal candidate is someone proactive, creative, reliable, flexible, and enthusiastic about working within a multicultural environment.  

ACCOUNTABILITIES / RESPONSIBILITIES* Manage the strategy, planning and logistics of all types of meetings, travel and events to support implementation of WFP Global Philanthropy strategy.
* Support the Global Philanthropy team to coordinate across projects including meetings with internal and external stakeholders.
* Facilitate communication within the team, Regional Bureaux and Global Offices, coordinating meeting schedules, agenda, note\-taking, circulation of next steps and taking ownership of follow\-up actions.
* Support the division in the coordination of the overall engagement strategy for ultra\-high\-net\-worth individuals and ‘billionaires’, including development of communication materials, presentations, and management of high\-level meetings and correspondence on behalf of the project lead and the Director.
* Conduct research, assist the screening of prospects and donors, support on due diligence process, for the development of the global philanthropy pipeline; identify key information such as philanthropic interests and areas of focus, engagement opportunities and peer to peer connections. Coordinate the use of WealthX for the global philanthropy team.
* Prepare internal and external\-facing presentations on MS PowerPoint and other applications/tools as needed to support organization of meetings, workshops, events and all\-hands sessions.
* Prepare first draft of appeals, take meeting minutes and assist with meeting facilitation when needed.
* Manage files and content in the Global Philanthropy SharePoint page, documents and confidential assets creating a secure repository for internal use.
* Ensure that divisional activities within your remit and oversight follow WFP Rules and Regulations. Other responsibilities as required.
* DELIVERABLES AT THE END OF THE CONTRACT
* Updated events calendar for the year (for Leadership team and PSP Management)
* Updated partner and prospect lists; Salesforce entries; SharePoint folder; and forecasts
* Drafting of reports, research, briefing and presentation materials, including presentations to new staff and to external partners.
* Reliable and timely organization of events, information, communications, confidential assets, and other materials, including consistent supervision of internal processes
* Efficient coordination of divisional activities.


QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE REQUIRED
Education* Completed University degree in Political Sciences, Business Management, International Relations, Intercultural Heritage and Identity, Languages and Cultural Mediation or related disciplines.


Work history* 3 to 5 years of relevant experience working in an administrative role, and/or project management experience is highly desirable.


Technical expertise* Advanced computer skills in Microsoft Office applications: proficiency in Word and PowerPoint
* Technically savvy with ability to use SharePoint and project management tool.
* Demonstrated ability to work in a fast\-paced environment.
* Strong time management, research \& analytical skills.


Soft skills* Highly organized with exceptional planning skills to deliver on commitments.
* Outstanding attention to detail.
* Ability to think creatively and strategically.
* Excellent communicator with ability to work collaboratively with colleagues across different cultures, disciplines, and time zones.
* Motivated and friendly team player with the ability to contribute to a gender\-balanced, inclusive, and sound working environment.
* Outstanding organizational skills, able to work to tight and multiple deadlines and prioritize workload.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with all levels of management and staff.


Language(s)* Excellent command of spoken and written English
* Intermediate knowledge of another official UN language would be a plus (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian, Spanish).


WFP LEADERSHIP FRAMEWORK  

WFP Leadership Framework guides to the common standards of behavior that guide HOW we work together to accomplish our mission.  

REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION  

WFP is committed to supporting individuals with disabilities by providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process. If you require a reasonable accommodation, please contact: global.inclusion@wfp.org
NO FEE DISCLAIMER  

The United Nations does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. Should you receive a solicitation for the payment of a fee, please disregard it. Furthermore, please note that emblems, logos, names and addresses are easily copied and reproduced. Therefore, you are advised to apply particular care when submitting personal information on the web.  

REMINDERS BEFORE YOU SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION* All applications must be submitted exclusively through our online recruiting system. We do not consider CVs or applications sent by email, LinkedIn, or any other channel.
* We strongly recommend that your Workday profile is accurate and complete, and that all sections are filled in, including your employment history, academic qualifications, language skills, and UN grade (if applicable). Once your profile is completed, please apply, and submit your application.
* If you experience technical issues while submitting your application, you may contact us at global.hrerecruitment@wfp.org. Please note that this email is only for technical issues with an application \- unsolicited applications or documents sent to this inbox will not receive a reply.
* At the application stage, the only required documents are your CV and Cover Letter. Additional documents (passport, certificates, recommendation letters, etc.) may be requested later in the process.
* Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited to proceed to the next stage of the recruitment process.
